addon\testdata\content15\tbl\HunterDataBase.mbm:
1070: [Color:0xc]◆Parallel Universes[Color:0x0]
Shin Megami Tensei IV: Apocalypse 
represents an alternative outcome than 
the one explored in Shin Megami Tensei IV. 
The point of divergence begins when 
SMTIV:A's protagonist meets Dagda. In one 
reality, the protagonist declines Dagda's 
offer and dies. Afterwards, events unfold
as witnessed in SMTIV.

A similar branching of realities occurs 
in SMTIV, too, depending on which of the 
ideologies Flynn embraced. In one reality, 
he sided with Merkabah and maintained the 
status quo by eradicating the Unclean Ones. 
In another reality, Flynn sided with Lucifer
and incited revolution, spreading the flames 
of war across Tokyo and created a world 
shaped by the strong. A third reality 
branched off if Flynn sided with no one,
and instead found his own path and removed
the dome of rock covering Tokyo. A fourth
reality splintered off if Flynn chose to
have the world obliterated by a black hole.

As illustrated, there are countless 
possibilities that can lead to countless 
alternative universes, each possibility 
possibly influencing another. Once ”observed,” 
each alternative universe becomes its own 
”true” reality. So in a way, there is no 
permanent end to this world.
[END]


addon\testdata\content16\tbl\HunterDataBase.mbm:
1080: [Color:0xc]Observation/Understanding[Color:0x0] 
As described by Stephen, ”observation” 
(sometimes described as ”understanding”) 
is the ability humans possess to give 
structure to the formless. In this 
instance, ”observation” has nothing to 
do with sight, but rather human perception. 
It is an extension of the philosophical 
concept of ”qualia,” or individual, 
subjective experiences. For example, 
the sensation of seeing color is not 
easily communicated, yet something 
humanity possesses a shared understanding 
of. Likewise, YHVH only exists because 
of this shared observation.

Of course, forms of communication are 
necessary in order for humans to share 
their observations with one another. 
But once framed by language, these 
concepts change in nature. In effect, 
they become bound by language, much 
like the shared understanding of YHVH.
What Dagda and the Divine Powers seek 
to do is free themselves from the 
restrictive, limiting existence forced 
upon them by language. Because YHVH 
never revealed himself to humanity, 
he is not bound by language the same 
way Dagda and the Divine Powers are.
[END]

addon\testdata\content24\tbl\HunterDataBase.mbm:
920: [Color:0xc]◆Summary[Color:0x0]
  An artificial gene angels inserted into 
  those who chose to live above the Ceiling.
[Color:0xc]◆Signs of Infection[Color:0x0] 
  The condition progresses in three stages. 
    1. Asymptomatic stage (incubation period)
      It is believed that 90% of Mikado's
      population carries the Demon Gene, 
      but it is asymptomatic unless certain 
      conditions are met.
    2. Onset
      *”Archangels are not nearby” 
      *”Infected have progressed to a certain
      point of cultural development” 
      Meeting the above conditions causes 
      the onset of the Demon Gene. Infection
      can also spread communally, without
      meeting the conditions stated above.
      Infection causes the reddening of
      eyes and the cerebral overactivity. 
      Excess dopamine causes hyperactivity
      and the feeding center of the brain, 
      compelling cannibalism.
    3. Complete infection 
      Further progression spreads from excessive
      cerebral activity to the rest of the body,
      causing transformation. This may cause
      increased strength and the development of
      abilities at great risk to the host's body.       
[Color:0xc]Prevention[Color:0x0]
  The Eastern Kingdom of Mikado's settlers
  were genetically modified by the angels.
  The Demon Gene since passed down from
  generation to generation. When people
  from Tokyo broke through the Ceiling and
  joined the Mikado population, the Demon 
  Gene was bred out of a small percentage.
  Theories suggest the contagion spreads
  through biological magnetite, but concrete
  details are too lacking to be sure.
  
[END]
930: [Color:0xc]◆Summary[Color:0x0]
  Hugo as transformed by the Demon
  Gene. Formerly the head of the
  Eastern Kingdom of Mikado's Monastery.
  (Please view entry on [Color:0x5]Hugo[Color:0x0] for more 
  details.)
  

  He is the first person to become 
  transformed by the Demon Gene. 
  After full onset, he becomes one with 
  YHVH's Universe and assumes the role 
  of God's messenger. He strived to 
  spread the Demon Gene. Those he 
  infected fell under his control. 
  Hugo also gains an intuitive insight into
  the location of those he infects. The 
  infected cannot be freed while the
  one controlling them still lives. [Flynn]
  eventually defeated Hugo, ending the
  threat of a Demon Gene epidemic.
[END]

addon\testdata\content29\tbl\HunterDataBase.mbm:
1020: [Color:0xc]◆Summary[Color:0x0]
A Domain constructed by En no Ozuno, regarded
as an echo of the Expanse. As such, time flows
differently within the domain. It is used as a
training ground for potential messiahs, but also
serves as a refuge for those seeking asylum from
the human realm.
[END]
1030: [Color:0xc]◆Summary[Color:0x0]
The 18−year−old protagonist of Shin Megami
Tensei I. Once a normal high schooler, his
destiny was forever changed when he found
himself caught in a war between gods and
demons.
[END]
1040: [Color:0xc]◆Summary[Color:0x0]
The protagonist of Shin Megami Tensei II.
Discovered by a trainer at Tokyo Millennium
and taught to be a gladiator.
[END]
1050: [Color:0xc]◆Summary[Color:0x0]
The 18−year−old protagonist of Shin Megami
Tensei III: Nocturne. Once an ordinary high
schooler, he was transformed into a demon
in the Vortex World, where he continues
to fight alone.
[END]
1060: [Color:0xc]Summary[Color:0x0]
Sometimes cooperates with those who pursue the
middle path, though he is a neutral party
himself. Stephen is not a normal human since
he is able to travel in the Expanse, the
Diamond Realm, and even dreams. Current
speculation states Stephen is a being from a
higher dimension who can influence parallel
universes. His real name is unknown−−”Stephen”
is said to be an assumed name.
[Color:0xc]Age/Sex[Color:0x0]
Unknown/Male
[Color:0xc]Activity[Color:0x0]
In 2038, Stephen hacked into the Ministry of
Defense and, with the use of the same technology
used to create the Yamato Perpetual Reactor,
developed the Demon Summoning Program. He then
distributed the program across the Internet to
aid humanity against the demon threat. Following
God's Plan, Stephen joined the Goddess of Tokyo
to help guide the city's messiahs toward the
middle path. Stephen's agenda remains unclear.

[Color:0x5]This entry may have been written by a
biased party.[Color:0x0]

addon\testdata\content32\tbl\HunterDataBase.mbm:
1020: [Color:0xc]◆Summary[Color:0x0]
  ...In process of writing
[END]
1030: [Color:0xc]◆Summary[Color:0x0]
  Protagonist of SMT 1. 
  ...In process of writing
[END]
1040: [Color:0xc]◆Summary[Color:0x0]
  Protagonist of SMT 2. 
  ...In process of writing
[END]
1050: [Color:0xc]◆Summary[Color:0x0]
  Protagonist of SMT 3. 
  ...In process of writing
[END]
1060: [Color:0xc]Summary[Color:0x0]
Sometimes cooperates with those who
pursue the middle path, though he is
a neutral party himself. It is evident
that Stephen is not a normal human,
since he is able to travel in the Expanse,
the Diamond Realm, and even dreams.
Current speculation is that Stephen is
a being from a higher dimension. This is
supported by his ability to influence
parallel universes. His real name is 
unknown−−”Stephen” is said to be an 
assumed name. Though he is always seen
in a wheelchair, it is believed his
legs are still functional.
[Color:0xc]Age/Sex[Color:0x0]
  Unknown/Male
[Color:0xc]Activity[Color:0x0]
Stephen created the Demon Summoming
Program in 201X, distributing it
indiscriminately throughout the Internet
as a means to combat the increasing
number of demons appearing across the
world. After God's Plan, he accompanied
the weakened Goddess of Tokyo and visited
Tokyo's would−be messiah, advising them
to follow the middle path. Only the
Goddess of Tokyo seems to be responsible
for awakening the messiahs, though details
are scarce. Similarly, it is unclear why
Stephen assisted the Goddess.

  [Color:0x5]This entry may have been written
by a biased party.[Color:0x0]
  [Color:0x5]*More Data Needed[Color:0x0]
[END]

[->]The shining turn icon means [Color:0x5]you
can perform another action[Color:0x0].
[->]Exploiting an [Color:0x5]enemy's weakness[Color:0x0]
rewards you with an additional action.
[->]In this instance, Katakirauwa's
weakness is physical attacks.
[END]
300: There are no more turn icons.
[->]That means it's now the enemy's
turn to attack.
[END]
301: As you can see, earning more actions
by exploiting enemy weaknesses is the
key to gaining the upper hand in battle.
[->]This time, the enemy weakness was
a physical attack. Other enemies will
have different weaknesses, however,
such as ice or electric skills.
[->]Much like you can gain actions,
you can also [Color:0x5]lose actions[Color:0x0].
[->]If your attack [Color:0x5]misses, or is
nullified[Color:0x0], you'll lose an action.
[->]This applies to enemies, as well.
[->]If the enemy exploits your weakness,
they gain an extra action. Conversely,
evading or nullifying an enemy attack
decreases their available actions.
[->]This concludes a basic overview of
combat. Keep an eye on enemy weaknesses
and the number of actions you can
perform to stay on top.
[END]

event\HowTo\HowTo.mbm:
10: Making contact with an enemy will initiate
combat.

If you press the X Button to attack enemies
before they touch you...
[END]
11: ...you get a preemptive attack at the start
of battle!

However, certain battles will not allow a
preemptive attack.
[END]
20: [Color:0x5]Press Turn Icons[Color:0x0]
The blue icons in the top right corner of
the top screen are called Press Turn Icons.
They represent the number of actions you
can perform this turn.
[END]
21: In general, the number of icons at the start
is equal to the number of your party members.

[Color:0x5]Flashing Icons[Color:0x0]
When you exploit an enemy's weakness or land
a critical hit, a Press Turn Icon will flash,
signifying that you can take an extra action.

[Color:0x5]Battle Tips[Color:0x0]
Exploiting enemy weaknesses and gaining more 
actions per turn is the key to victory. 
Juggling basic attacks and skills with elemental 
properties such as Ice and Elec will let you hit
multiple enemies' weaknesses.

But just as you can gain actions, you can also
lose them. If your attack misses or is nullified,
you'll lose more than just one Press Turn Icon,
leaving you at a disadvantage.

The same applies to enemies. If an enemy hits
your weakness, they'll gain more actions. If
you dodge their attack or nullify it, however,
the enemy will lose more actions.

This concludes the flow of battle overview.
Keep an eye out for enemy weaknesses, be mindful
of your own, and look for opportunities to turn
the tide of battle in your favor.
[END]
30: When [Flynn] levels up, you can improve his stats.

You can allocate the stat points you earn by
leveling up any way you want.
[END]
31: There are five stats: Strength, Dexterity, Magic,
Agility, and Luck.

[Color:0x5]Strength[Color:0x0]
Affects the power of normal attacks and Phys skills.

[Color:0x5]Dexterity[Color:0x0]
Affects the power of gun attacks and skills.

[Color:0x5]Magic[Color:0x0]
Affects the power of magic skills.

[Color:0x5]Agility[Color:0x0]
Affects attack accuracy, evasion, turn order, 
and chance of escaping from battle.

[Color:0x5]Luck[Color:0x0]
Affects things like critical hits and status
ailment recovery.
[END]
40: Relics are obtained from certain spots on the
field map. Relics can be sold to weapon and
item shops for Macca.

Relics are found by investigating buildings 
and piles of debris on the ground.
[END]
41: After sufficient time has passed, new relics
appear in spots you've already searched before,
so always be on the look out for more.
[END]
50: Tap the Mapper icon on the bottom screen to
open the app.
[END]
51: Mapper lets you survey the area and helps guide
you to your current destination. Quest objectives
are displayed on the top screen.

Mapper has four main features:

[Color:0x5]Current Location[Color:0x0]
Centers the map on your current position. 
Press the Y Button to trigger this view.

[Color:0x5]Destination[Color:0x0]
Centers the map on your destination, marked with
a red flag. Press the X Button to view it.
[END]
52: [Color:0x5]Zoom[Color:0x0]
Tap − to zoom out, + to zoom in. Pressing the
A Button also switches between these two views.

[Color:0x5]Change Floors[Color:0x0]
Tap ↑ to show the next floor up, ↓ to show the
next floor down. You can cycle through floors by
pressing Up and Down on the +Control Pad too.
[END]
60: When you leave an area, you're taken back to
the world map. Several Mapper features are
only available for use on the world map.

[Color:0x5]Toggle Location Names[Color:0x0]
Turn location names on or off. 
Toggle with R Button.

[Color:0x5]Change Display[Color:0x0]
Cycle through map indicators, such as relics
or key locations.

Current Location and Destination behave the
same on the world map as they do in areas.
[END]
70: Certain actions, like exploiting an enemy's
weakness, will grant you the Smirk status.
[END]
71: When Smirking, your attacks have a 100% hit
rate and are always critical hits.

Additionally, enemies can't land critical hits
on you, nor exploit your own weakness.

Smirk ends after you've attacked an enemy, 
after several turns, or if you're inflicted
with a status ailment.

The most common ways to gain the Smirk status are:
 − Exploit an enemy's weakness
 − Land a critical hit
 − Nullify, repel, or drain an enemy's attack
[END]
80: Unlock new Apps by spending App Points.
[END]
81: Earn App Points by leveling up or by using
certain items.

Some Apps become available through leveling.
Others open up by unlocking specific Apps.

Newcomers might consider starting with these 
four apps:

[Color:0x5]Analyze[Color:0x0]
View demon data during battle. Weaknesses will
show for any demons you've fought before or
have had in your party.

[Color:0x5]Skill Expansion[Color:0x0]
Grants [Flynn] an additional skill slot, allowing
him to have more skills at once.

[Color:0x5]Demon Skill+[Color:0x0]
Grants demons an additional skill slot, allowing
them to have more skills at once.

[Color:0x5]Expand Stock[Color:0x0]
Adds an additional slot to your demon stock.
You cannot recruit demons if your stock is
full. The Expand Stock app increases your
stock space by one.
[END]
90: StreetPass lets you attach a demon to a DDS
(Digital Demon Service) Card and exchange
them with other players. These exchanges
can yield several benefits.
[END]
91: There are two kinds of DDS interactions:
− StreetPass
− Internet

[Color:0x5]StreetPass[Color:0x0]

  [Color:0x5]Growth Type[Color:0x0]
  Stats of attached demon increased based on
  number of StreetPass exchanges made.

  [Color:0x5]Fusion Type[Color:0x0]
  Attached demon will fuse with other player's
  demon and become a new one. Be mindful not
  to attach a demon you don't want to change.

[Color:0x5]Internet[Color:0x0]
By selecting ”DDS” and then ”Internet”
to connect online, you can upload your DDS
Card and download the DDS Cards of other players.

After downloading a DDS Card through the
Internet, you won't be able to download
again for two hours of cumulative game time.
[END]
100: During battle, use Talk to access the 
Scout option. Successfully Scouting a demon 
allows you to use them as allies in battle.
[END]
101: There's a pattern to what responses demons prefer,
so observe their speech patterns closely to look
for clues. Even if negotiation fails, a demon
might take a liking to you if you continue to
speak with them.

Having a conversation consumes Press Turn Icons.
The amount varies depending on the outcome of
the conversation.

You can only recruit a demon through Scouting
if you have room in your stock and if the demon
is the same level as you or lower. There are
select demons that cannot be recruited even if
these conditions are met.

Also, you cannot recruit a demon if you already 
have the same one in stock.
[END]
110: When a demon's learned all its hidden skills,
it can pass along whispered skills.
[END]
111: A whispered skill is one the demon can share 
with the protagonist. Learning whispered skills
helps make the protagonist stronger. Some demon
skills cannot be passed on, however.

When the protagonist already knows a whispered
skill, that skill gains an upgrade. Each skill
has an upgrade limit, however, and once reached
the upgrade number shown on screen will turn from 
green to yellow.

[Color:0x5]Demon Skills and Affinities[Color:0x0]
Demon skills cannot be strengthened. Instead,
demons have innate affinities for specific
types of skills. The higher their affinity
is, the better they are at those skills.

If a demon's affinity is at the max
level for that skill type, the number
shown on screen will be yellow.
Conversely, if it is at the lowest
level, the number will be magenta.

You can check a demon's skill affinities on
the status screen.
[END]
112: [Color:0x5]Changing Skill Order[Color:0x0]
To change the order skills are listed,
select ”Skill Order” from the ”Party” menu.
[END]
120: Partners are characters that aid you and your
demons in battle.
[END]
121: Partners act at the end of your turn. While
you can't choose their actions, they will
perform a variety of skills to help support you.

Be warned: If your attack is repelled or drained,
causing you to lose your turn, your partner's turn
will be skipped, too.

Like the protagonist, partners have their own HP.
If their HP reaches 0, they're left unable to act.
After several turns, however, they'll rejoin the
fight.
[END]
130: [Color:0x5]Main Partner[Color:0x0]
Your Main Partner will assist you in battle.
There can only be one Main Partner at a time.
You can change your Main Partner from the
”Partner” menu.
[END]
131: Every partner has their own style of support
and set of skills. Selecting the partner that
most suits your playstyle is another way to
help turn the tide of battle in your favor.
[END]
140: Challenge Quests are automatically registered
to your phone whenever specific conditions are
met. These quests can be completed at any time.
[END]
141: Completing Challenge Quests gains you additional
experience points along with a reward, so it's wise
to take these on between main story moments.

There are five types of quests−−delivery, combat,
travel, achievement, and training−−each with their 
own specific conditions.
[END]
142: [Color:0x5]Delivery Quests[Color:0x0]
Quests that require a specific item or demon
be delivered to a Hunter Association.

[Color:0x5]Combat Quests[Color:0x0]
Quests that require a specific demon or group
of demons be defeated.

[Color:0x5]Travel Quests[Color:0x0]
Quests that require you to reach a specific
location.

[Color:0x5]Achievement Quests[Color:0x0]
Quests that are completed once you reach a
specific record. Progress is tracked in the
Play Log.

[Color:0x5]Training Quests[Color:0x0]
Quests that require you to meet an objective
using specified conditions. There is no penalty
for failure, so these quests can be tried as
many times as you want until you beat them.
Access Training Quests by accepting them from
the Challenge Quests list.
Occasionally, you will receive additional 
rewards for completing Challenge Quests 
in addition to your normal rewards.
[END]
150: Force skills and select special skills have
a low chance of causing your demons to become
Lost.
[END]
151: Demons with the Lost status are forcibly removed
from battle, and cannot be resummoned until they're
found.

Whenever you get into a new encounter, there's
a chance the Lost demon will appear. When it
does, the demon will rejoin your stock.
[END]
160: Examining Power Spots fills the Jade Dagger 
with spirit energy.
[END]
161: Once filled, pressing the X Button will cause
Navarre to charge forward and attack with the
Jade Dagger. This can do two things:

[Color:0x5]Destroy Wraith Walls[Color:0x0]
The Jade Dagger can destroy Wraith Walls that
block the way.
[END]
162: [Color:0x5]Repel Enemies[Color:0x0]
The Jade Dagger can be used against enemies
that are a lower level than the protagonist
to prevent an encounter.

The Jade Dagger's spirit energy drains as
you move. Once the spirit energy runs out,
you won't be able to perform either action.
[END]
170: Assist Attacks interrupt an enemy turn, gaining
you an additional one. After your partners use
support skills, they all team up to deliver a
massive attack.
[END]
171: The Assist Gauge increases with each action
your partner takes during battle. Once full,
an Assist Attack will trigger at the end of
the turn.
[END]
180: Some areas contain traps that restrain the
protagonist.
[END]
181: Escape from traps by rapidly pressing the X
Button. If you don't escape in time, you'll be
transported to a different part of the area.
[END]
190: [Color:0x5]Messiah Blast[Color:0x0]
Flynn and the protagonist can team up to unleash
a powerful skill called Messiah Blast.
[END]
191: Messiah Blast can only be performed when Smirking.

To activate Messiah Blast, choose ”Flynn” from
the battle menu during the protagonist's turn.
[END]
200: In YHVH's Universe, Flynn will support the
protagonist in various ways.

[Color:0x5]Enemy Removal[Color:0x0]
When Flynn sees an enemy, he will run up and 
defeat it.
[END]
201: [Color:0x5]Macca and Items[Color:0x0]
Flynn will collect Macca and items from the
enemies he defeats. Speak with him to receive
anything he collected.
[END]
202: [Color:0x5]Disabling Traps[Color:0x0]
Flynn will free the protagonist from any traps
he gets caught in.

If Flynn is fighting a different enemy, the
”Flynn” option won't be available during combat.
After several turns, Flynn will rejoin you.
When he does, the Messiah Blast option will
be available again.
[END]
210: When fighting YHVH, you will alternate between
the protagonist's party and Flynn's party.

Healing and support effects only apply to the
active party.

Similarly, Flynn can only access items he
himself is carrying.
[END]
220: During Smirk, some skills will gain an additional
effect. These effects vary depending on the skill.

For example, during Smirk, Hama and Mudo gain
the chance to cause an instant kill.

Enemies also gain these effects during Smirk.
[END]
230: You can save and change difficulty from the 
System menu.

[Color:0x5]Saving[Color:0x0]
The first slot saves to the Game Card,
while slots 2 through 5 save to the SD card.
For the downloadable version of the game,
all slots save to the SD card.

[Color:0x5]Difficulty[Color:0x0]
There are three difficulty levels: Skirmish,
Conflict, and War. Conflict is the standard 
difficulty.

Story and quest rewards do not change based
on difficulty level. Choose whichever most
suits your personal skill level.
[END]

10: [Color:0xc]Hunter Name[Color:0x0]

[Flynn] now operates as an official

[END]
11: [Color:0xc]History (Addendum)[Color:0x0]
Prior to becoming an official Hunter,
[Flynn] died in a demon attack, but
was resurrected after striking a deal with
Dagda. [Flynn] is also responsible for
releasing Krishna, retrieving the
Ame−no−habakiri, collecting components for
the Shesha Radar, and defeating Shesha
itself.
It is unclear whether [Flynn]'s
actions are of his own free will or the
manipulations of the demon Dagda.
[END]
12: [Color:0xc]History (Addendum 2)[Color:0x0]
While [Flynn] was at Cafe Florida,
the Divine Powers spread their demon
forces throughout Tokyo and without
warning, attacked the underground
districts simultaneously. Fujiwara
agreed to let [Flynn] prove his
innocence by helping repel the demon
invasion. [Flynn] succeeds. Following
the demon attack, [Flynn] next
dispelled the Tokugawa Mandala, a
magical barrier exploited by the
Divine Powers to block the use of
the Demon Summoning Program.
[END]

21: [Color:0xc]History (Addendum)[Color:0x0]
Lost her father when the Divine Powers sent
their demons to attack the underground
districts. There is no other living family
listed on record. Currently, her father's
successor at the Kinshicho Hunter Association
is registered as her legal guardian.
[END]
22: [Color:0xc]History (Addendum 2)[Color:0x0]
Though killed by the demon serpent Shesha,
Asahi was returned to life upon Krishna's
defeat. She has since resumed duties as an
active Hunter.
[END]
30: [Color:0xc]Summary[Color:0x0]
A student in training for service
as an active, official Hunter.
[Color:0xc]Becoming a Hunter[Color:0x0]
In order for a cadet to advance to
full Hunter status, they must first
apprentice under an appropriately
ranked Hunter. Following extensive
training, the Hunter must then
recommend the cadet to the Hunter
Association.

Alternatively, if a Hunter application's
achievements and accomplishments can
be assessed as equal or superior to
standard training, they might be given
official Hunter status. Few, however,
have become a Hunter through these
means.

As for the Cadet experience, training
varies greatly between instructors.
There is no formal curriculum. The
work asked of Hunters spans a wide
range of skills, though some Hunters
have been known to specialize.
[END]
40: [Color:0xc]Hunter Name[Color:0x0]
Nikkari
[Color:0xc]Sex[Color:0x0]
Male
[Color:0xc]Home District[Color:0x0]
Kinshicho
[Color:0xc]History[Color:0x0]
Operated in official Hunter capacity
through the Kinshicho Hunter Association.
Specialized in delivery−type quests.
One of the most reliable Hunters and
respected by his peers, Nikkari was a 
passionate instructor, often taking cadets
into the field with him during quests.
In the days of God's Plan, Nikkari served
with the JSDF stationed at Camp Ichigaya.
There, he met the future founders of the
Hunter Association, Fujiwara and Skins.
[Color:0xc]Death[Color:0x0]
During a routine foraging mission just
outside of Kinshicho, Nikkari was killed
by a demon serving Lucifer.
[END]

Though technically a novice Hunter,

Nikkari, his former mentor.

outside of Kinshicho, Manabu was killed
by a demon serving Lucifer.

60: [Color:0xc]Summary[Color:0x0]
Ironic name of artifacts made before God's Plan.
[Color:0xc]Importance of Relics[Color:0x0]
Following God's Plan, both the acquisition and
manufacture of goods dropped precipitously.
These relics proved to be a valuable resource
to the people of Tokyo, who collect them daily.
[Color:0xc]About the Name ”Relics”[Color:0x0]
Supposedly, the name for these artifacts has
origins with the Samurai who came down from
the Ceiling and had been tasked by their kingdom
to collect these ”mystic relics”. When the
Samurai used this name while buying and selling
the artifacts, Tokyo denizens found it amusing
and perpetuated this wording.
[END]
70: [Color:0xc]Summary[Color:0x0]
A hand−held computer and cell phone
that is capable of running a variety
of applications.
[Color:0xc]Functions[Color:0x0]
− Calls
− Send/Receive Emails & Texts
− Creating Documents
− Managing Info (schedules, addresses, etc.)
− Camera
− Clock
− Calculator

Additional functions can be added
by installing new apps. For Hunters,
these apps are included by default:

− Quest Management
− Skill Mangement
− Item Management
− Party Management
− Demon Summoning Program
− Cathedral of Shadows
− Demon Analysis
− GPS
− Report Missing People
− Various Databases
[END]
80: [Color:0xc]Summary[Color:0x0]
Prior to God's Plan, domesticated mammals
(primarily cows and pigs) provided the main
source of protein within Tokyo. Because of
the severe shortages in livestock, however,
demon meat is used as a replacement. Demons
fit for human consumption are categorized
as the ”Food” race.
[END]
90: [Color:0xc]Summary[Color:0x0]
Formerly Japan's peacekeeping military
organization, responsible for national
defense, maintaining public order,
and disaster relief. The JSDF has since
disbanded, and its members scattered.
[END]
100: [Color:0xc]Summary[Color:0x0]
Once a term used to describe the
anthropomorphized versions of natural
phenomena or, in certain cultures,
minions of evil. Presently used to
describe the non−terrestrial life−forms
that appeared after God's Plan. The term
encompasses a variety of creatures across
a spectrum of cultural backgrounds.
[Color:0xc]Races[Color:0x0]
There are over fifty known demon races,
identified by appearance and origin.
[Color:0xc]Emergence[Color:0x0]
Modern reports of demons place their
emergence in the 1980s, though at the
time their presence was so low they
were often dismissed as urban legend.
After the incident with the Yamato
Perpetual Reactor in which a gate to
the Expanse was created, the demon
population surged. Following God's
Plan, reports of humans turning into
demons also began to surface, though
the cause for this remains unknown.
One theory asserts that the magnetite
found naturally in all humans is
somehow able to react to will and
desire, sometimes triggering said
transformation. If true, this theory
would mean that all humans are then
capable of becoming demons. The bulk
of this theory comes from reports
about the human consumption of Reds,
but because evidence of Red−induced
transformation into demons is so rare,
the theory remains speculative at best.

[Color:0x5]*More Data Needed[Color:0x0]
[END]
110: [Color:0xc]Summary[Color:0x0]
A cascade of wars and economic collapse
that spread across the world in 2013,
following the Yamato Perpetual Reactor
incident that resulted in the opening
of a gateway to the Expanse. This
incident coincides with the digital
distribution of the Demon Summoning
Program. As a result of both events,
thousands of demons appeared throughout
the world, instigating global chaos.

The ensuing wars would see over a
hundred nuclear missiles launched all
across the world, spread so that no
surface on Earth would be left
unscorched. The wars taxed the global
economy $1.7 trillion dollars and
caused an estimated 7.2 billion
casualties. The exact number remains
unknown.
[Color:0xc]Tokyo During the Missile Launch[Color:0x0]
The worldwide simultaneous launch of
nuclear missiles included Tokyo, but
just before the city was struck, the
dome of rocks now referred to as the
Ceiling appeared, preventing any
damage.
[Color:0xc]Unexplained Mysteries Surrounding God's Plan[Color:0x0]
A great many mysteries persist from
God's Plan. The Demon Summoning Program,
for instance, remains an enigma. Who
created it? Why? Though many theories
exist, a definitive answer has yet to
be found. The details surrounding the
global missile launch also remain unknown.
When the war ended, and the angels
appeared, many ascribed responsibility
to them. This is why the event came to
be known as God's Plan.
[END]
120: [Color:0xc]Summary[Color:0x0]
The guardian deity of Tokyo.
Also one of the National Defense Divinities.

Transformed into a dome of rock to prevent
nuclear missiles from striking down on 
the city.
[Color:0xc]Masakado's Life[Color:0x0]
During the mid−Heian era, he claimed himself
the ”New Emperor” and led a rebellion to
liberate the Kanto region. Branded a traitor
by the court, he was eventually killed by
his ally, Fujiwara no Hidesato, and Taira
no Sadamori. Masakado appears in numerous
legends, sometimes even referred to as the
”Guardian of Tokyo” in honor of his stand
against the tyranny of the court and efforts
to create a better future for the people.
[END]
130: [Color:0xc]Summary[Color:0x0]
A program designed to simulate and simplify
rituals and acts necessary to summon demons.
Put simply, the program puts the power to
summon up demons in the hands of even the
most ordinary of people. Though, whether
they are able to control the demon is
entirely up to them. Inexperienced users
are often overpowered by the demon and
either killed or fall under the control of
the demon. Neither the identity of its
creator nor the purpose behind its design
are known. Hunters are awarded smartphones
equipped with the Demon Summoning Program
upon registration as an official Hunter.
[Color:0xc]Relation to God's Plan[Color:0x0]
It is widely believed that the war now
called God's Plan erupted as a result
of the Demon Summoning Program's
distribution.
[END]
131: [Color:0xc]The Tokugawa Mandala's Influence[Color:0x0]
The Tokugawa Mandala set up by the Divine
Powers disabled use of the Demon Summoning
Program. Fortunately, [Flynn] shattered
the seal and restored the app's functionality.
[END]
140: [Color:0xc]Summary[Color:0x0]
Shortly after God's Plan ended, a small
group managed to breach the Ceiling through
the Sky Tower. There they encountered the
Angels of Death. Despite years of effort,
the group was unable to break through the
Angels of Death, forcing them to retreat
back to Tokyo.
[END]
150: [Color:0xc]Summary[Color:0x0]
As seen in the Eastern Kingdom of
Mikado's legends and lore, the name
for residents of the ”Underground
Country” (read: Tokyo).
The ”Unclean Ones” were thought to
be little more than myth, the stuff
of fairytales. However, once Flynn
and his comrades journeyed to Tokyo,
many are starting to acknowledge the
city as the one referred to in legends.
[Color:0xc]Usage[Color:0x0]
There is increasing call to end the
use of the term, as it is regarded
by the people of Tokyo as a slur.
[END]
160: [Color:0xc]Summary[Color:0x0]
Officially known as the ”Hachibu Rengou
Ashura−kai,” the organization HQ is in
Roppongi, but they maintain branches
throughout every underground district.
The Ashura−kai's main goal is to maintain
order and protect citizens from demons.
The safety of the underground districts
partly stems from their efforts. However,
some Ashura−kai perform their duties
through extortion, demanding unreasonable
compensation in exchange for protection,
and even use violence. For a time, the
districts could do little about this. But
once Tayama vanished, the organization's
power weakened and its enforcers dwindled.
[END]
161: [Color:0xc]Acting Leader (Addendum)[Color:0x0]
At present, a man by the name of Abe has
stepped up as interim leader while Tayama
remains missing. Through him, the Ashura−kai
are managing to barely hold together.
[END]
162: [Color:0xc]The New Boss (Addendum 2)[Color:0x0]
During Armageddon, a young man by the name
of Hallelujah declared himself the Ashura−kai's
new leader. Since then, his support has only
increased. Great things are expected of him.
[END]
170: [Color:0xc]Summary[Color:0x0]
Former Ashura−kai leader who brought
relative order to Tokyo by negotiating
with the demons. Whereabouts unknown.
[Color:0xc]Age/Sex[Color:0x0]
48/Male
[Color:0xc]History[Color:0x0]
Prior to God's Plan, Tayama was little
more than a common gangster. He rose
to power in the chaos caused by the
sudden appearance of demons. Tayama
is responsible for establishing the
underground districts. He is said to
wield one of the National Defense
Divinities.

Tayama is currently missing. Rumors
of his death persist, but to date
no body has turned up.
[END]
180: [Color:0xc]Summary[Color:0x0]
A religious group operating out of
Tsukiji Konganji. Their principle
doctrine is ”survival of the fittest,”
be they demon or human. The Ring
of Gaea used to be rivals of the
Ashura−kai, but after their leader's
death, the group at large has weakened.
[Color:0xc]Survival of the Fittest[Color:0x0]
The Ring of Gaea's dogma is modeled
upon natural selection, but is often
confused by its members to mean a 
”dog eat dog” philosophy. The teachings
of Yuriko were the root of this violence.
She instructed her followers to unleash
their suppressed emotions upon the world
to shape it as they saw fit. While it
stands to reason that this may have
been Yuriko's intent all along, her 
machinations remain a mystery.
[END]
181: [Color:0xc]Factional Schism (Addendum)[Color:0x0]
When the Divine Powers appeared, they usurped
Tsukiji Konganji from the Ring of Gaea. As a
result, a factional rift within the Ring of
Gaea formed, dividing its members between
followers of Yuriko and those of the Divine
Powers' Maitreya. Maitreya is believed to
have played a crucial role in Tsukiji
Konganji's takeover.
[END]
182: [Color:0xc]Maitreya Faction Mass Suicide (Addendum 2)[Color:0x0]
During a raid on Tsukiji Konganji in an
effort to rescue Flynn and defeat the
Divine Powers, Hunters discovered that the
Ring of Gaea members loyal to Maitreya
committed mass suicide by feeding themselves
to Shesha. Because of this, the Yuriko faction
met no resistance when reclaiming the area.
[END]
190: [Color:0xc]Summary[Color:0x0]
Former leader of the Ring of Gaea.
True identity is the demon Lilith.
Defeated by Flynn.
[Color:0xc]Age/Sex[Color:0x0]
Unknown/Female
[Color:0xc]History[Color:0x0]
There are no records of Yuriko prior to
God's Plan. When the war ended, however,
Yuriko created the Ring of Gaea in
opposition of the Ashura−kai. Yuriko
led the Ring with one specific philosophy:
survival of the fittest. Concealing her
face behind armor of a black demonica suit,
Yuriko managed to infiltrate Mikado and
create a stir there. She was killed by
one of the Kingdom's Samurai−−a man named
Flynn, who came to Tokyo hunting for her.
[END]
200: [Color:0xc]Summary[Color:0x0]
Facilities within each underground
district that serve as hubs for the
Hunter Association. The organization
itself was founded by Fujiwara and Skins.
[Color:0xc]Role[Color:0x0]
Each Association manages registered
Hunters by providing support, rewards,
and assignments, ensuring that the right
jobs end up with the right Hunters.
[Color:0xc]Founding[Color:0x0]
It is widely believed that founders
Fujiwara and Skins created the
organization to prepare for another
attempt at breaching the Ceiling.
[END]
210: [Color:0xc]Summary[Color:0x0]
Officially appointed members of the
Hunter Association who make their
living by killing demons and taking
on requests for help.
[Color:0xc]A Hunter's Work[Color:0x0]
Hunters are chiefly responsible for
procuring provisions (food, relics),
patrolling the underground districts,
helping direct trade traffic, and
transporting goods. Assignments are
determined by a Hunter's experience.
Upon completing requests, Hunters are
immediately rewarded and their exploits
logged in the Hunter Association database.
[Color:0xc]Relationship Between Hunters and Demons[Color:0x0]
Most Hunters use the Demon Summoning
Program, completing their assignments
through the use of demons. 

There are some, however, resolved to
only use their own strength. But for
you, the Association recommends using
the app and summoning demons.
[Color:0xc]Official Appointment[Color:0x0]
To become a Hunter, one must first
serve as a cadet and undergo training
with an experienced Hunter. After
sufficient training, the instructor
must then recommend their cadet to
the Hunter Association.
[END]
220: [Color:0xc]Summary[Color:0x0]
The colloquial term for the missions
assigned to or accepted by Hunters.
These are typically assigned through
the Hunter Association itself, though
sometimes Hunters receive work directly
from a client.

Missions managed by the Association are
marked by a category tag to help specify
the type of assignment.

The Hunter Association files their
assignments under one of nine categories:

Assist [Ast]
Guard [Grd]
Rescue [Rsc]
Intel [Int]
Investigate [Inv]
Escort [Esc]
Target [Tgt]
Special [Spc]
Other [Otr]
[END]
230: [Color:0xc]Summary[Color:0x0]
The designation given to demons that
serve humans and are controlled by
the Demon Summoning Program.
[END]
240: [Color:0xc]Summary[Color:0x0]
One of the Hunter Association founders.
[Color:0xc]Age/Sex[Color:0x0]
50/Male
[Color:0xc]History[Color:0x0]
Prior to God's Plan, Fujiwara served
as a journalist in the Counter−Demon
Force. After Tokyo became sealed within
the dome, Fujiwara cooperated with Skins
to burrow through the Ceiling. On the
other side, they encountered the Angels
of Death and were forced back down into
Tokyo. After that, Fujiwara and Skins
founded the Hunter Association. Fujiwara
continues to oversee operations, where
he focuses on protecting the people
of Tokyo.
[END]
250: [Color:0xc]Summary[Color:0x0]
One of the Hunter Association founders.
[Color:0xc]Age/Sex[Color:0x0]
55/Male
[Color:0xc]History[Color:0x0]
Prior to God's Plan, Skins served
in the Counter−Demon Force. When the
Ceiling appeared over the city, Skins
helped Fujiwara burrow through it using
the Sky Tower. Once through, they
encountered the Angels of Death, and
were forced back down to Tokyo.

After that, Fujiwara and Skins founded
the Hunter Association. Skins continues
to oversee operations, where he focuses
on protecting the people of Tokyo.
[Color:0xc]Masakado's Katana[Color:0x0]
Until recently turning the blade over to
Flynn, Skins had been in possession of
Masakado's Katana since God's Plan ended.
In all that time, Flynn was the first
person who proved able to free the sword
from its sheath. Most believe that part
of the reason Skins carried the sword
with him was in remembrance of his dead
friend, the man who served with him in
the Counter−Demon Force.
[END]
260: [Color:0xc]Summary[Color:0x0]
A young Samurai from Mikado. He is burdened
with many expectations, having been dubbed
”Liberator” and ”messiah” by those in Tokyo.
[Color:0xc]Age/Sex[Color:0x0]
18/Male
[Color:0xc]History[Color:0x0]
Flynn is from above the Ceiling, a place
near Mikado called Kiccigiorgi Village.
When he turned 18, Flynn was selected as a
Samurai as part of a traditional coming−
of−age ritual. As a Samurai, Flynn pursued
the Black Samurai (later identified as
Yuriko) into Tokyo. He became entangled in
the affairs of both the Ashura−kai and the
Ring of Gaea, but favored no one side over
the other and chose to find his own way.
Between all this, and his high−ranking 
Hunter status, Flynn has become known as
the ”Liberator” of Tokyo.
[END]

Expanse's official currency. Before God's

Tokyo became encased in the rock dome, its
citizens began to use Macca to barter with
demons. Its use has since become common.

291: [Color:0xc]Akira's True Intentions (Addendum)[Color:0x0]
Akira's decision to stay above the Ceiling
was not, in fact, an act of betrayal, but
rather an attempt to deceive the angels in
order to form the Eastern Kingdom of
Mikado. As King Aquila, he unified the
new nation and created a safe place for
the other humans above the Ceiling. Mikado
endured under the authority of the angels
until Armageddon. It now maintains an
amicable alliance with Tokyo, and people
from both lands travel freely between the
two. It seems that Akira's original
intentions have come to fruition.
[END]

321: [Color:0xc]Current Status (Addendum)[Color:0x0]
Following the Cosmic Egg's appearance,
reports indicated that [Flynn]
had taken possession of the sword.
There's been no indication that he
is also able to wield it, however.
To date, only two people are known
to have freed the sword from its
sheath: Skins' companion from the
Counter−Demon Force and Flynn.
Unverified reports suggest there may
be some connection between the two.

Assuming [Flynn] can in fact draw
the sword, it would suggest that
this connection, this physical
resemblance, is not what determines
whether someone can unsheath the
blade. Unfortunately, because so
few people have been able to wield
the sword, the requirements to do
so remain unclear.

350: [Color:0xc]Summary[Color:0x0]
Soldiers of the Eastern Kingdom of Mikado who
protect the land from demons. Only those
chosen by the Gauntlet Rite become Samurai.
[Color:0xc]Role[Color:0x0]
The primary duties of Samurai are to defend
Mikado and acquire relics. In many respects,
Samurai are analogous to Hunters. Both wield
demons in battle and have similar tasks. It's
unclear if other factors differentiate them.
[Color:0xc]The Gauntlet Rite[Color:0x0]
There is a tradition within Mikado that,
upon turning 18, everyone must participate
in the Gauntlet Rite, a test that selects
Samurai based on whether the smartphone−like
Gauntlet device (one that bears striking
resemblance to Counter−Demon Force demonica
tech) they wear activates or not. Those
chosen to be Samurai relocate to Mikado
Castle.
[END]
351: [Color:0xc]Relationship to Akira (Addendum)[Color:0x0]
King Aquila, founder of the Samurai order
in Mikado, is the adopted name of the Hunter
Akira. This explains the similarities
between the Hunter Association and the
Samurai, since the very first Samurai were
made up of Hunters who stayed with Akira in
Mikado. New research has determined that
those able to activate the Gauntlet during
the Rite are descendants of the original
Samurai/former Hunters.
[END]
360: [Color:0xc]Summary[Color:0x0]
A nation set atop the Firmament. Mikado
is a castled city that is situated above
the former Kanto region. It was founded
by King Aquila roughly 1500 years ago.
[Color:0xc]Social System[Color:0x0]
Mikado had maintained a feudal caste
system during the era of the first king
to King Ahazuya. The two castes within
Mikado were the upper−class Luxurors and
the lower−class Casualries. However, the
caste system was dissolved with the
downfall of King Ahazuya.
[Color:0xc]Military Strength[Color:0x0]
Since Mikado lacks any enemy kingdoms,
the Samurai order is solely dedicated
to dealing with demons. Every citizen
of Mikado, Luxuror and Casualry alike,
is tested when they turn 18 to determine
whether they're Samurai. Only a handful
are chosen each year, so the Samurai
order only numbers in the low hundreds.
[Color:0xc]Time Dilation[Color:0x0]
According to its history, Mikado has
existed for 1500 years. The dome the
nation is built upon, however, only
appeared 25 years ago. The difference
is caused by time flowing differently
in Tokyo and Mikado.
[END]
361: [Color:0xc]Time Dilation Negated (Addendum)[Color:0x0]
The time differential between Tokyo and
Mikado is 1:75, meaning that every six
months in Tokyo amounts to six and a half
years in Mikado. When Shesha appeared and
burst through the Ceiling, however, the
differential disappeared. Much debate has
been had over the cause of the time
dilation, but nothing beyond theories
have been produced. The phenomenon is
still being researched.
[END]
370: [Color:0xc]Summary[Color:0x0]
A bracer worn by Mikado's Samurai.
It functions a lot like smartphones,
complete with Demon Summoning Program
and map navigation. And like the phones
Hunters use, Gauntlets also have onboard
AI support.
[Color:0xc]About Support AI (Addendum)[Color:0x0]
Gauntlets are installed with the ”Burroughs”
operating system. She provides various
support features essential to Samurai.
[END]
380: [Color:0xc]Summary[Color:0x0]
A Japanese coming−of−age ceremony
that dates back to to Japan's Nara
Period (710−794 AD). Youths typically
underwent the ritual between the ages
of 10 and 20. The ceremony itself
draws inspiration from a Tang Dynasty
Chinese custom. The name has since
faded out of fashion, but the ceremony
itself is still celebrated in Japan
as the Coming−of−Age Day festival,
which became a national holiday in
1948.
[END]
390: [Color:0xc]Summary[Color:0x0]
Lightly battered and deep fried poultry.
Chicken was among the most commonly consumed
meats, but is incredibly difficult to come
across now. Gryphon, Caladri, and Chupacabra
meat (the cheapest option) are all used as
substitutes.

Fried chicken differs from cook to cook.
Some prefer it crispy, and so use potato
starch. Others prefer a fluffier texture,
and use flour in the mix. For something in
between−−which is best for packed lunches−−
the starch and flour can be blended to
prevent moisture from escaping.
[Color:0xc]Side of Lemon[Color:0x0]
Traditionally, fried chicken is served
alongside lemon juice, which gives the
chicken a lighter, more refreshing taste.
The added lemon is also thought to help
with digestion and with blood sugar.
Fried chicken lovers can be quite picky
about whether they use lemon juice.
More than a few friendships have ended
when someone poured lemon juice over
fried chicken without asking first.
[END]
400: [Color:0xc]Summary[Color:0x0]
An ex−Samurai from Mikado. Brought
to Tokyo by his fellow Samurai, Flynn.
Later discovered dead, having drowned.
[Color:0xc]Age/Sex[Color:0x0]
25 (at death)/Male
[Color:0xc]History[Color:0x0]
A member of Mikado's Luxuror class,
Navarre was chosen to be a Samurai when
he was 18 during the Gauntlet Rite.
He was eventually removed from the
Samurai order due to an undisclosed
”incident.”

Navarre actively opposed the abolition
of Mikado's caste system, and in doing
so put his life in danger of retribution
from the angels. But with Flynn's help,
Navarre managed to flee Mikado and take
refuge in Tokyo.

Not long after his exile, however,
Navarre was discovered dead in the Kanda
River. In the absence of any foul play,
it is believed Navarre took his own life.
[Color:0xc]Age Discrepancy[Color:0x0]
Though Flynn and Navarre became Samurai
at the same age, Navarre is now seven
years older than Flynn. This was caused
by Flynn's activity in Tokyo, while
Navarre remained in Mikado.
[END]

420: [Color:0xc]Summary[Color:0x0]
Tech that makes near−instant transport
of physical objects from one point to
another possible. Housed in Terminal Rooms.
[Color:0xc]Details[Color:0x0]
Teleportation is achieved by sending
objects between Terminals. The only
limitation is that the item being
transported fit within the Terminal
Room itself. As for how they work,
well, that remains a mystery.
[Color:0xc]Installation[Color:0x0]
The Terminals were constructed as a
means of transporting people and
equipment up the Sky Tower while
tunneling up through the Ceiling.

However, after encountering the Angels
of Death, all Terminals were powered
down. They've since been reactivated
by Flynn. All Terminals are currently
active and available for use, pending
user registration.
[END]

430: [Color:0xc]Summary[Color:0x0]
An app designed to streamline the reportage
of missing persons. The app is included by
default on phones distributed to many
Hunters.

Whereas in the past, people would need to
report a death to a government agency and
go through a bureaucratic process,
post−government Tokyo instead relies on
a database maintained by the Hunter
Association.

The MIA App streamlines this documenting
and even facilitates contact with known
family.
[Color:0xc]Use in the Field[Color:0x0]
The MIA App is a normal part of everyday
Hunter life. Whenever a Hunter comes
across the body of a fallen Hunter, they
can use the MIA App to photographically
identify the deceased and register the
information.

The MIA App also handles the transfer of
app points (AP) from the deceased to the
Hunter who found them, as a form of
reward.
[END]

540: [Color:0xc]Summary[Color:0x0]
Tamagami, the former defense minister,
created the Counter−Demon Force to fight
the growing tide of demons. Their main
headquarters was in Kasumigaseki. The
group was active all throughout Japan
but concentrated in the Kanto region.
The group was disbanded following
the collapse of the government.
[Color:0xc]Role[Color:0x0]
The Counter−Demon Force was tasked
with minimizing damage caused
during demon−related incidents
and provide defensive assistance
to the populace. Members of this
unit were chosen for their mastery
of summoning demons, regardless of
age or gender. Tamagami envisioned
the Counter−Demon Force as the world's
most elite demon fighting squad.
[Color:0xc]Equipment[Color:0x0]
The Counter−Demon Force wore battle
suits called ”Demonicas.” Each
Demonica was equipped with an advanced
AI.
[END]
550: [Color:0xc]Summary[Color:0x0]
An alliance of demons, angels, and
humans created to fight the Divine
Powers.

Merkabah represented the angels.
Lucifer represented the demons.
Fujiwara and Skins represented humanity.
A Samurai of the Eastern Kingdom of
Mikado, Isabeau, served as a mediator.
[Color:0xc]First War Council[Color:0x0]
The first council introduced a truce
between the angels and demons.
Merkabah pledged to send an elite team
of Samurai to Tokyo to aid in the fight
against the Divine Powers. Lucifer 
declared that the people of Tokyo would
do his bidding in the upcoming war.
Humanity was unable to have much of an
influence during the negotiations.
[END]
551: [Color:0xc]Second War Council (Addendum)[Color:0x0]
The second council served to reassess the
situation after the defeat of Shesha.
[END]
552: [Color:0xc]Third War Council (Addendum 2)[Color:0x0]
The Third War Council dissolved the truce
between the angels and demons and led
directly to the events of Armageddon.
[END]
560: [Color:0xc]Summary[Color:0x0]
A Hunter Association R&D engineer.
[Color:0xc]Age/Sex[Color:0x0]
60/Male
[Color:0xc]History[Color:0x0]
While the details are unclear, Matsuda was
allegedly a video game programmer before
eventually being scouted by Fujiwara to
join the Hunter Association's research
and development team.
[END]
570: [Color:0xc]Summary[Color:0x0]
The reactor that supplies electricity to
Tokyo. Ever since it started functioning,
it has provided energy to the city without
fail, but no one is sure exactly how it
works.
[Color:0xc]Cause of ”God's Plan”[Color:0x0]
The initial influx of demons during the
events known as ”God's Plan” were thought
to be caused by the reactor creating a
gate connecting this world to the Expanse.
[Color:0xc]Original Intent[Color:0x0]
The Yamato Perpetual Reactor was originally
created as a particle accelerator. It is
theorized that it could be capable of
producing a black hole which would
completely swallow Tokyo.

590: [Color:0xc]Summary[Color:0x0]
A member of the Ashura−kai. He is like
a brother to Abe.
[Color:0xc]Age/Sex[Color:0x0]
15/Male
[Color:0xc]History[Color:0x0]
Hallelujah was adopted into the Ashura−kai
by Tayama when he was an orphan and has
been a member ever since. Nowadays, he
patrols the streets of Tokyo with the
interim leader, Abe.
[END]
591: [Color:0xc]Leader of the Ashura−kai (Addendum)[Color:0x0]
After the death of Abe during the
Armageddon, Hallelujah declared himself
the leader of the Ashura−kai.

Under normal circumstances, many steps
are usually taken to acquire this
position. Notably, there are some
agreements made by higher−ups in the
organization about the successor, but
since the previous leaders, Tayama and
Abe, have gone missing or are dead, this
appointment has not been contested.
Having killed the duplicitous, previous
leader, Hallelujah has the most legitimate
claim to this responsibility.
[END]
592: [Color:0xc]Leader of the Ashura−kai (Addendum 2)[Color:0x0]
Hallelujah has ordered the Ashura−kai
to participate in the rebuilding of
Tokyo and demanded an end to all
extortion performed by the group.
[END]
600: [Color:0xc]Summary[Color:0x0]
A demon of the Divine Powers.

Medusa controlled the Sky Tower, 
petrifying and collecting those who
tried to get past the Ceiling, until
she was defeated by Flynn as he entered
Tokyo. She was resurrected by the Divine
Powers only to be defeated by [Flynn],
who was there to complete a quest.
[Color:0xc]Sky Tower[Color:0x0]
The Sky Tower's current appearance is due
to Medusa creating a multitude of domains
in the Tower in an effort to strengthen
it.
[END]
610: [Color:0xc]Summary[Color:0x0]
A Samurai from the Eastern Kingdom of
Mikado. Chosen by Merkabah to lead the
Crusaders into Tokyo.
[Color:0xc]Age/Sex[Color:0x0]
18/Male
[Color:0xc]History[Color:0x0]
A Luxuror from the Eastern Kingdom of
Mikado who was recently selected to be
a Samurai during the Gauntlet Rite.

While he is a new Samurai, Gaston has
innate talent and impressed Merkabah.

Thus, he was chosen personally by Merkabah
to be the leader of the Crusaders and
was bestowed with the Spear of Michael,
a weapon that was created to destroy
Shesha. Gaston carries the expectations
of many people on his shoulders.
[END]
611: [Color:0xc]Parting Ways with Merkabah (Addendum)[Color:0x0]
Upon facing Merkabah during Armageddon,
he was asked to cooperate, but he refused
and broke the Spear of Michael that
Merkabah had given him.
[END]
620: [Color:0xc]Summary[Color:0x0]
The spear that Merkabah gave to Gaston,
the Crusaders leader. Apparently, the
shape of the spear changes according to
the will of the wielder.
[END]
621: [Color:0xc]Properties (Addendum)[Color:0x0]
Eyewitnesses claimed to have seen the
spear vastly increase in size upon
puncturing Shesha's hide, but no
concrete explanations for this
phenomenon have been given. Some
surmise the spear adapts to the will
of the one bearing it, while others
think the spear reconstructs matter.
[END]
622: [Color:0xc]The Broken Spear (Addendum 2)[Color:0x0]
Gaston shattered the spear to dissolve
his alliance with Merkabah. Considering
the durability of the weapon, it is hard
to imagine how a human managed to break
such a powerful artifact. It has been
suggested that the spear became fragile
in the same way that Gaston's allegiance
to Merkabah had become.

[Color:0x5]*More Data Needed[Color:0x0]
[END]
630: [Color:0xc]Summary[Color:0x0]
A device that can pinpoint Shesha, the
giant serpent unleashed by the Divine
Powers. Shesha has the ability to freely
traverse space, which made tracking it
very difficult for angels and demons.
However, it was discovered that Shesha
emitted waves when creating rifts in
space to travel between locations.
This discovery led to the creation of
these radars, which are being distributed
to Hunters everywhere.
[Color:0xc]Receivers and Transmitter[Color:0x0]
The devices given to Hunters are actually
receivers for the main radar, which is
located underground in Kasumigaseki.

The primary radar is an enormous piece
of equipment constructed in the Hibiya
utility corridor. While most radars receive
and measure sound waves to determine the
distance and direction of a target, this
construct looks at gravitational waves.
To properly detect these waves, the radar
must be located in a stable environment,
so the creator constructed it 131 feet
below the surface, while building it to
last and be simple to control.
[END]
631: [Color:0xc]Destruction[Color:0x0]
The Shesha radar housed in the Hibiya
utility corridor was destroyed by an
unknown party. However, the device is no
longer necessary since Shesha and the
Divine Powers are no longer a threat to
Tokyo. Thus, repairs have not been made.
[END]
640: [Color:0xc]Information[Color:0x0]
A group of Samurai elites that Merkabah
organized with the goal of defeating the
Divine Powers and getting Flynn back.
The leader is a Samurai named Gaston.
He is a young, skilled Samurai that was
hand−picked by Merkabah to lead the group.
[Color:0xc]Group Composition[Color:0x0]
Five of the unit's Samurai are designated
fighters, while 12 other Samurai are
tasked with gathering intelligence.
Gaston is grouped with the fighters,
but likes to do things on his own.
[END]
641: [Color:0xc]Disbanding[Color:0x0]
Once the Crusaders acheived their goal
of defeating the Divine Powers, the group
disbanded. All of these Samurai were
ordered to return to Mikado, but some
of them wanted to stay and ignored this
order.
[END]

690: [Color:0xc]Summary[Color:0x0]
One of the National Defense Divinities used
to defend Tokyo. He was under Tayama's
control before he went missing. Tenkai was
tasked with guarding Midtown, but became
a mountain of stone after losing to Flynn.
[Color:0xc]History[Color:0x0]
He was on the staff of Tokugawa Ieyasu,
the first shogun of the Edo shogunate.
He helped with policy and city planning
and built the Tokugawa Mandala according
to onmyo−do and feng shui to help with
the defense of the country.
[END]
700: [Color:0xc]Summary[Color:0x0]
Powerful demons that were summoned during
God's Plan to resist invasion by foreign
countries and demons. They all have a
strong will to protect Japan. They swear
loyalty to anyone who possesses their
corresponding medium. Also, these deities
can be resurrected as many times as
necessary as long as their medium is
intact, even if they get defeated or
turned to stone. It is confirmed that
Tayama was in possession of several of
these mediums before Flynn came to Tokyo.
[Color:0xc]Confirmed Members[Color:0x0]
These eight demons have been confirmed to
be National Defense Divinities:

1: Koga Saburo
2: Tenkai
3: Michizane
4: Omoikane
5: Yamato Takeru
6: Yaso Magatsuhi
7: Take−Mikazuchi
8: Masakado
[END]
701: [Color:0xc]Newly Confirmed Divinity[Color:0x0]
A new National Defense Divinity has been
discovered!

9: Izanami

Izanami is a deity of the afterlife
held in a living medium. Upon her
medium's death, she was summoned.
Almost as soon as she was summoned,
she was defeated by a Hunter called
[Flynn].
[END]
710: [Color:0xc]Summary[Color:0x0]
Biological magnetism created by human
brainwaves. It is a form of energy that is
necessary for demons to take physical shape.
[Color:0xc]Demons[Color:0x0]
Spiritual beings like demons can
consume magnetite to maintain their
physical presence. If they wish to
remain corporeal, they must continue
consuming magnetite.

Demons that are summoned through the
Demon Summoning Program can consume
magnetite through their summoner, but
demons without a summoner must acquire
it in some other way. This factor is
why wild demons attack and eat human
beings.
[Color:0xc]Reds[Color:0x0]
Reds produced by the Ashura−kai have
a high concentration of biological
magnetite. The ingredients and process
to create Reds has been lost with
their manufacturing facility.
[END]
720: [Color:0xc]Summary[Color:0x0]
A magic barrier that the Divine Powers
set up. It is based on a spiritual seal
that Tenkai put up in Edo 400 years ago.
The Divine Powers were able to stop the
Demon Summoning Program from working by
utilizing the seal.
[Color:0xc]Effects[Color:0x0]
The Tokugawa Mandala is activated by jars
placed in the five Fudo temples absorbing
Aether infinitely. This Aether is the
same thing as biological magnetite. By
having the jars absorb the Aether coming
from the Expanse, it prevents demons
from appearing in our world. The types
of Aether and the temples that regulate
them are as follows:

Blue (Kyoseiin): Sloth
White (Hakujoin): Gluttony
Red (Nansekiji): Lust
Black (Ryukokuji): Wrath
Yellow (Okuji): Pride
[END]
730: [Color:0xc]Summary[Color:0x0]
A member of the Ring of Gaea.
She seems to be sent on missions to
destroy things or assassinate people.
[Color:0xc]Age/Sex[Color:0x0]
14/Female
[Color:0xc]History[Color:0x0]
Toki's parents were both devoted to
the Ring of Gaea, and Toki was given
to the Ring when she was small.
Mii and Kei, long−time black operatives
for the Ring of Gaea, trained her to
be an assassin.
[END]
731: [Color:0xc]Leaving the Ring of Gaea (Addendum)[Color:0x0]
Upon destruction of the Divine Powers,
Tsukiji Konganji was returned to the
Ring of Gaea. Toki declared then that
she would leave the Ring of Gaea. She
currently works with [Flynn].
[END]
740: [Color:0xc]Summary[Color:0x0]
The ”Lady of Heaven,” she was able to gain
physical form by consuming the Aether from
the jars the Divine Powers used to set up
the Tokugawa Mandala. Defeated by [Flynn].
[END]
741: [Color:0xc]Resurrection (Addendum)[Color:0x0]
She was supposed to have been defeated by
[Flynn] when the Tokugawa Mandala was
destroyed, but it seems she possessed
Toki, who was there at the time. 
Because of Toki's role in the unsealing
of the Tokugawa Mandala, it is thought
that a lot of Aether had accumulated
in her body. Inanna consumed this Aether
to gain strength. Because of Inanna's
resurrection, the Divine Powers were
able to spawn demons endlessly, but
she was defeated by [Flynn] and Toki
was safely freed.
[END]
750: [Color:0xc]Summary[Color:0x0]
A ship that was found deserted in the
Atlantic Ocean in 1872. There were
supposed to be ten people on the ship,
but none were located. To this day,
no one is certain where those people
went. The strange part is that when
the ship was discovered, the interior
looked as if people had just been there.
For example, a cup of tea was found on
a dining table, reportedly still warm.

While many people have suspicions
about how these people disappeared,
no substantial evidence has surfaced,
making this one of the greatest nautical
mysteries in history.
[Color:0xc]Fantastical Dramatization[Color:0x0]
The bit about the tea still being warm
was probably made−up. The current story
is filled with so many creative additions
that it's practically entered the status
of being an urban legend at this point.
[END]
760: [Color:0xc]Summary[Color:0x0]
The first king of the Eastern Kingdom of
Mikado. He was known to be a skilled fighter
and possessed a wealth of information far
surpassing the standards of the time.
[Color:0xc]Age/Sex[Color:0x0]
Unknown/Male
[Color:0xc]History[Color:0x0]
The first mention of Aquila in historical
texts appeared when the people of Tokyo
attempted to breach the Ceiling. The
Unclean Ones were at war with the Angels
of Death, but Aquila appeared suddenly
and put an end to the fighting. After
this, he founded the Eastern Kingdom of
Mikado and established a centralized
government. Aquila also created the
Samurai to prevent anyone from Tokyo
entering his kingdom. The pre−existing
Monastery was so pleased by his efforts
that they granted him even greater powers.
But a few years later, King Aquila
disappeared. No one is certain why, but
some suspect an assassination occurred.
[END]
761: [Color:0xc]About Aquila (Addendum)[Color:0x0]
New information reveals that Aquila was,
in fact, a member of the Counter−Demon
Force named Akira. He based the Samurai
on the CDF and conspired against the angels
in Mikado. He planned to seal the four
archangels. This is believed to have led
to his death.
[END]
770: [Color:0xc]Summary[Color:0x0]
The head monk of the Monastery in the
Eastern Kingdom of Mikado. He informs
the populace of Merkabah's will.
[Color:0xc]Age/Sex[Color:0x0]
Late 40s/Male
[Color:0xc]History[Color:0x0]
A Luxuror from the Eastern Kingdom of
Mikado. He was chosen to be a Samurai
at age 18 during the Gauntlet Rite.
He was always better with words than
with physical combat, and became close
to King Ahazuya of Mikado by taking
advantage of his own eloquence.
He was promoted at an astounding
rate, and became the head monk at
a remarkably young age. Hugo was second
in power only to the king, but lost
much of his influence when the angels
took over Mikado. He now acts as their
servant.
[Color:0xc]Tastes[Color:0x0]
Hugo is famous for being a connoisseur
of fine wines. Wine is not made in the
Eastern Kingdom of Mikado, so one must
procure it from Tokyo.

He sometimes sends personal requests
for wine to Samurai going to Tokyo.
[Color:0xc]Spouse[Color:0x0]
Hugo is also famously afraid of his
wife. She is an assertive woman who,
in Hugo's eyes, asks the impossible of
him. It's not unusual for her errands 
to turn into missions for the Samurai.
[END]

the Eastern Kingdom of Mikado.
[Color:0xc]Age/Sex[Color:0x0]
Early 50s/Male
[Color:0xc]Information[Color:0x0]

Mikado. He is a skilled warrior and one
of the strongest men to ever lead the
Samurai, rivaling even K. While still
active in the field, Hope is starting
to focus on mentoring and training the
younger generation of Samurai.
[END]

After founding Maruology, he has been

[Color:0xc]About Maruology[Color:0x0]
Maruology's philosophy revolves around
living moment to moment just as you are.
There are many among its ranks who are
ex−Ashura−kai or Ring of Gaea members.
He attracts many followers who are all
about self−acceptance in the form of
stealing food and being obnoxiously loud.
Not too surprisingly, this group has
its fair share of critics. His followers
now number in the hundreds, so Maruo's
influence can no longer be ignored.

870: [Color:0xc]Summary[Color:0x0]
The defense minister of Japan during
God's Plan. He went missing after the
Yamato Reactor, which was housed under
the Ministry of Defense, went haywire.
[Color:0xc]Age/Sex[Color:0x0]
68 (at disappearance)/Male
[Color:0xc]History[Color:0x0]
Previously a part of the Japanese Air
Self−Defense Force, Tamagami eventually
turned his attention to politics and was
appointed to the position of Minister of
Defense right before God's Plan. With
the cooperation of an undisclosed foreign
nation, he fostered development for the
cutting−edge Demonicas to combat demons.
But at the same time, Tamagami had the
Yamato Reactor created without anyone
else in the goverment knowing. On the
surface, it was a secret infinite energy
project. But in reality, the Yamato Reactor
was drawing energy from the Expanse. 
However, the reactor malfunctioned and
an overflow of demons surged into this
world, causing the events of God's Plan.
He was last seen in front of the Yamato
Reactor before his disappearance.
[END]

Works at the Hunter Association as an
expert in data management. Not many
have met her in person. The action
reports of Hunters are sent to the
Association database and are saved as
logs. Betty evaluates these logs and
determines rank among the Hunters.

the meat is sworn to secrecy.

1: We did it! We're finally Hunters!
Dad sees us as more than just kids,
now! If only Nikkari and Manabu were
here to see this... We'll just have
to work twice as hard in their honor!
They'd be happy to know we became
successful Hunters, right?
[END]
2: Flynn is sooo cool! That angel would've
wiped the floor with us if he didn't
show up and kick its butt like it was
nothing. That's why he's our Liberator!
But you know who really blew my mind?
Miss Isabeau. She's so strong, and so
graceful... I want to be like her.

I don't think Dad would've made us
Hunters if they hadn't said all those
nice things about us.

Come to think of it... They really
helped us out, but we didn't help
them any. Okay, we need to get stronger
so that the next time we see them, we
can help. Right, Nanashi?
[END]
3: Hey Nanashi, is it just me or are you 
talking to yourself all of a sudden? 
Are you okay? You stressed or something? 
I know a lot's happened recently.

You know you can talk to me about
anything, so just let me know, okay?
[END]

responsibility Dad had. If I'd known
how much work was involved, I wouldn't
have acted the way I did with him. Still,
I feel like I'm getting to know him
better, doing this. It's hard work, but...
I'm happy. I even started learning how
to be a bartender. It's not half bad!

None of this means I won't keep working
as a Hunter with you, Nanashi!
[END]
74: You all have been so active as of late.
It seems you've really taken my teachings
to heart. I fear I might be the only one
standing still...

Perhaps I should find a hobby for
myself. Since I'm a ghost, I suppose
I could take to haunting people.
Though, no one owuld be able to see
me, would they...

Why don't you find a fitting hobby
for me! I'm counting on you, my top
pupil!
[END]

rescuing me, [Flynn].

I am confident that the two of us
will find a way to break humanity
free of YHVH. Let us finish this
fight together. I vow to protect
you to my last breath.
[END]

and Merkabah. I'll transfer that to you
now.

status\profile.mbm:
6: Dummy Text
[END]
10: The prince of darkness in Judeo−
Christian lore, known for his role as
the snake that tempted Adam and Eve at
Eden. It is also said he is sent by God
to test man's piety.
[END]
11: Sometimes called the ”heavenly chariot”
or the ”throne of God.” Described as
having four faces and four wings in
Ezekiel.
There is also a form of meditation
that invokes Merkabah used to
connect to a higher realm. The ritual
is quite dangerous, so caution is
advised.
[END]
12: The highest rank of all the angels, it
is the being closest to God.
Humans can rarely detect their presence,
but they are said to have four heads and
six wings. They also appear in human form.
[END]
14: The greatest and most mysterious of the
angels.
He has many names, such as the Voice of
God and Angel of Contracts. In contrast
to his duty to maintain the world, he is
said to have a merciless side toward
humanity.
[END]
16: The angel who persecutes evil in Hebrew
folklore. He carries out punishments
for God.
He tempts humans and tests their faith.
He asked God to permit him to have
demons as his subordinates. In the
Zadokite Fragments and the Dead Sea
Scrolls, he is the angel of disaster,
the father of all evil, and a flatterer
of God.
[END]
17: He is the angel of beauty, also known as
Hanael. His name means ”grace of God.”
He is generally associated with the
planet Venus and performs a similar role
as the goddess Ishtar.
He gives rise to feelings of love
amongst people and has the role of tying
together young men and women. He is also
thought to be the symbol of lasting
friendship and lust. When he appears,
it is said to be in the form of a
beautiful man or woman.
[END]
18: In Zoroastrian lore, Sraosha's duty is to
listen to humanity's cry for Ahura Mazda.
Also known to descend after sunset to
vanquish evil. Its name literally means
”observance.”
[END]
20: The Islamic angel of death, said to have
four faces and four wings.
Azrael has a book in which he inscribes
and erases the names of those who are
born and die. His body is covered in
eyes. When one blinks, someone in the
world dies.
[END]
21: The sinful ”angel of the oath.” His
original name was Biqa, meaning ”good
person.”
He attempted to coerce Michael to tell
him the sacred name of God, but was
denied. After the Fall, he was given
the name Kazbiel, ”he who lies to God.”
[END]
23: The Islamic angel of music. It is said
that he will sound the trumpet during the
Last Judgment.
He has four wings and is said to tower
into the heavens. Thrice daily and
nightly, he sheds enormous tears while
watching humans tormented in Hell.
[END]
24: The guardian angel of Saint Patrick who
spoke to him in a dream and had him
spread Christianity throughout Ireland.
His role is to speak the will of God
to those who are to become leaders.
[END]
25: An angel in Judeo−Christian lore that
presides over conception.
She is the intermediary between pregnant
women and God. When a woman conceives
a child, the seed is given to God, who
then decides the child's sex, appearance,
and destiny. After such things are
decided, a new soul is placed into the
woman's womb.
[END]
31: In Hindu lore, the goddess of beauty
and good fortune.
Vishnu's wife and Kama's mother, she is the
goddess of love. She embodies the ideal
woman and is said to have charmed many
gods with her dance.
[END]
32: Goddesses of fate in Norse lore. They
live below the roots of Yggdrasil and
weave the threads of fate, which even
the gods are bound by.
[END]
33: The goddess of birth and death. She is
Baal's sister and wife.
It is said that even her father El in
the heavens feared her power. She is
known for bringing Baal back from the
land of the dead by going there herself
and defeating its ruler, Mot.
[END]
34: The Aztec goddess of filth. She is also
the goddess of sexual deeds, as well as
a goddess of purification.
She is also known as Tlaelquani (”she
who eats filth”), befitting her nature
as a goddess of atonement.
[END]
35: The virgin war goddess who sprung from
Zeus' forehead fully armored after he
swallowed her mother, Metis. She is the
patron goddess of Athens and is
associated with olive trees.
[END]
36: The Akkadian goddess of love and war.
She is known as the ”Queen of Heaven”
and is equated with the planet Venus.
In the epic of Gilgamesh, she had many
lovers, but all were fated to be killed
or turned into animals at her hands.
[END]
37: The war goddess of Celtic lore, as well
as the queen of the Land of Shadows.
She is a skilled magician and a master
warrior. She trains young men who come
to her in the art of war. The famed Cu
Chulainn was one of her students, and it
is said that he received the legendary
Gae Bolg once he mastered her teachings.
[END]
39: This beautiful goddess of love is one
of Shiva's wives in Hindu lore.
She is always at Shiva's side and played
a role in opening his third eye.
[END]
40: The Roman goddess of luck, she turns
the Wheel of Fortune.
She is believed to have originally been
a fertility goddess. Her Greek
counterpart is Tyche.
[END]
41: The Egyptian goddess of fertility. She
is also the goddess of love, motherhood,
and joy.
She is depicted as a cow with the disc
of the sun between her horns, or as a
horned woman holding the disc of the sun.
[END]
42: The goddess of fire in Irish lore. She
also presides over craftsmanship,
intelligence, poetry, and healing.
She is depicted carrying a magic cup
symbolizing a brazier. She had such
authority that her name itself was
synonymous with ”goddess.” When
Christianity spread to Ireland, she
was so revered by the masses that the
church canonized her as a Christian
saint.
[END]
43: A Mother Earth goddess of Japanese lore,
she gave birth to the islands with her
consort Izanagi.
She also gave birth to many gods, but she
died of burns when bearing the fire god
Hi−no−Kagutsuchi. Izanagi traveled to
Yomi no Kuni (the land of the dead) to
bring her back to the land of the living,
but because she ate the food of Yomi,
she was no longer able to return. When
Izanagi lit a torch and saw her rotting
body covered with maggots, he ran in
fear, and after that Izanami became the
goddess of the dead, Yomotsu−Okami.
[END]
44: Dummy Text
[END]
47: A divine bird−man in Hindu lore.
He once fought the gods and received
immortality in exchange for becoming
Vishnu's carrier.
[END]
48: A divine creature in Japanese lore.
They are three−legged birds sent by
Amaterasu to help humans.
They are said to have helped Emperor
Jinmu claim victory.
[END]
49: One of the Four Holy Beasts of Chinese
lore.
It represents the south, summer, and
fire. It is a giant bird that resembles
a quail and is said to chirp in five
beautiful voices.
[END]
50: A legendary bird found in Native
American lore.
Thunderbirds are said to live high in the
mountains. Their flapping wings cause
winds, their claps cause thunder, and
their eyes shoot light. It is said they
can carry the ocean on their backs and
eat whales whole.
[END]
51: A shining rooster in Norse lore that
sits at the top of Yggdrasil, the World
Tree.
Its name means ”tree snake,” and it
releases light atop Yggdrasil. By
basking in that light, Yggdrasil floats
into the sky.
[END]
52: The famous holy Egyptian bird that's
born from fire.
The Phoenix is a crimson−colored bird
the size of an eagle with a colorful
tail. After about 500 years, it throws
itself into a pyre and is reborn from
the ashes as a chick.
[END]
53: A legendary bird of Hindu lore. Its name
means ”one with beautiful wings.”
It can create wind at will by flapping
its wings, and can also change its
size freely.
[END]
54: The sacred white goose that Brahma rides
in Hindu lore.
The sight of pure white flying the winter
skies symbolizes the efforts of the
Brahmins trying to obtain Brahma's
knowledge. It is said that is why Hamsa
became Brahma's steed.
[END]
60: The ash tree known as the World Tree in
Norse lore.
Its branches cover the entire earth, and
its trunk pierces the sky and holds up
several worlds, including Alfheim,
the abode of the Light Elves. Its
three roots stretch into Jotunheim,
Niflheim, and Asgard. It is said that
it will live through Ragnarok.
[END]
61: A Persian god of Zoroastrian origin who
has a plant of the same name. He is the
giver of life.
The plant was originally a sacred plant
used to make stimulants. Unfortunately,
it is no longer known which herb that
was. It was used commonly in religious
rituals.
[END]
62: The god of trees in Japanese lore. Also
pronounced Kugunochi.
Along with Toyouke−Hime, Kukunochi was
a guardian of houses and is honored in
house−raising events.
[END]
63: An Aztec goddess said to have brought
the maguey plant to this world. As
granddaughter of a Tzitzimitl, she
descended to Earth alongside
Quetzalcoatl, disguised as the branches
of a forked tree. Her upset grandmother
found her, tore her to pieces, and ate
her, leaving Quetzalcoatl intact. Later,
Quetzalcoatl buried Mayahuel's bones,
from which the first maguey plant
sprouted.
[END]
64: A young man of Greek lore. He rejected
